body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 70/2026
Made under: Wildlife Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 70/2026, deposited April 27, 2026, under theWILDLIFE ACT[sections 64.1 and 109] andOFFENCE ACT[section 132]. Order in Council 165/2026, approved and ordered April 27, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that (a)	section 12 (c) of the Designation and Exemption Regulation, B.C. Reg. 168/90, is repealed, (b)	section 4 (2) of the Guiding Territory Certificate Regulation, B.C. Reg. 115/2015, is repealed, (c)	the Permit Regulation, B.C. Reg. 253/2000, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 1, and (d)	the Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation, B.C. Reg. 89/97, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 2. — R. NEILL,Minister of Water, Land and Resource Stewardship; N. SHARMA,Attorney General; J. WHITESIDE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 2 (c) (iii) of the Permit Regulation, B.C. Reg. 253/2000, is amended  by striking out "the wildlife resource" and substituting "a wildlife resource". (a)	in subsection (2) by adding the following paragraphs: (d)	for a purpose described in subsection (4) (a) to (f), (h), (i) or (j), (i)	section 11 (1) or (8), 33.1 (1) or (2), 35 (2) or 78 of the Act, (ii)	section 10, 11 or 12 of the Hunting Regulation, B.C. Reg. 190/84, (iii)	section 3.06 of the Wildlife Act Commercial Activities Regulation, B.C. Reg. 338/82, and (iv)	sections 23.011, 23.02 or 23.03 of the Wildlife Act General Regulation, B.C. Reg. 340/82; (e)	for a purpose described in subsection (4) (a) to (d), (g), (h), (i) or (j), section 17.1 (2) or 18 (1) (o) or (r) of the Hunting Regulation; (f)	for a purpose described in subsection (4) (a) to (d), (h), (i) or (j), section 27 (4) of the Act or section 18 (1) (p) or (q) of the Hunting Regulation; (g)	for a purpose described in subsection (4) (a) to (d), (h) or (j), section 3.05 of the Wildlife Act Commercial Activities Regulation., and (b)	by adding the following subsection: (4)	The purposes for which a regional manager may issue a permit under subsection (2) (d), (e), (f) or (g), as applicable, are the following: (c)	if the regional manager considers it necessary for the proper management of a wildlife resource;

minutes_briefs.title: BC updates wildlife permit rules and fines for resource management
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has amended several regulations under the Wildlife Act, including changes to the Permit Regulation and the Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation. The amendments update the language around wildlife resource management and expand the reasons a regional manager can issue a permit. These changes affect how permits are granted and how fines are administered for wildlife-related violations.
